mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months during sustained FEDFUNDS declines, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above its recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates compress and markets price earlier policy support; this combination predicts negative near-term SPX returns because inflation surprise dominates the easing benefit.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average by at least 0.3pp while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 5-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates weakening household income and slower earnings growth, which should pressure equity prices and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when labor deterioration is persistent.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are positive in a majority of UNRATE spike episodes, or if the signal's effect vanishes after the first month of the spike.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains above trend signal stronger demand and better earnings breadth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as firms sustain revenue momentum.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by at least 150k over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of strong payroll-growth episodes, or if the relationship reverses when unemployment is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad final demand and current output momentum, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal is not stronger than either series alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns fail to exceed 55% when both series are above their 3-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production reflects a supply-side recession and deteriorating corporate output, which should weigh on equities and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of consecutive-decline episodes, or if the effect is indistinguishable from noise after the second month.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall sharply, the decline signals tightening credit demand and weakening cyclical activity, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 10% from a 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of housing-drop episodes, or if the effect disappears once the decline is reversed.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP contraction combined with rising unemployment identifies a broad recession regime; under that regime, equities should continue to underperform over the next quarter because both output and labor income are deteriorating together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is below its prior-quarter level and UNRATE is rising
failure_condition: Reject if recession-triggered 60-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of cases, or if the joint signal is not stronger than the separate GDP and unemployment effects.
